title: hygrophila ringens var. assurgens
intro: Hygrophila ringens var. assurgens, also known as the Indian waterweed or hydrilla, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It is widely used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Hygrophila ringens var. assurgens include reducing inflammation, treating various infections, and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
